steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: King's Cross suggests to Margaret “infinity” and “eternal adventure”—deliberately opposed to ordinary materialism.

steps.title: Apply the signal
steps.content:
Choice A places King's Cross as a gateway to the countryside's “intangible abundance” and “authentic engagements”—the values Text 2 identifies as the novel's idealist opposition to London's capitalism.

The others misread plainness as capitalist or conflate it with disillusionment.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
